vulnerability.title: Set SSH Daemon LogLevel to VERBOSE
vulnerability.description: The VERBOSE parameter configures the SSH daemon to record login and logout activity. To specify the log level in SSH, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: LogLevel VERBOSE
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*LogLevel\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*LogLevel\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*LogLevel\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" cp "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode

triage_decision.reason: Adjusting SSH log level to VERBOSE is a standard hardening practice that enhances audit logging without altering authentication, authorization, or service functionality, posing minimal risk of service disruption.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Low risk: only increases log verbosity, no impact on SSH access or system stability.
